message:
test(search): add 34 planner unit tests for facets, ranking, scope, and edge cases (br-3vwi.10.1)

Sections 17-30: agent/project LIKE fallback, cursor×method interactions,
ranking×method matrix, explain output, direction×agent exhaustive matrix,
combined scope+facets+cursor, search scope enforcement, LIKE param
propagation, time range edges, redaction presets, visibility caller-scoped
matching, param ordering, determinism across doc kinds. Total: 126 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-3vwi.10","title":"[track] Comprehensive V2 test matrix (unit/snapshot/PTY/E2E)","description":"## Purpose
Define and implement a comprehensive quality net so V2 can evolve quickly without regressions, including parity obligations for TUI, web UI, and static export surfaces.

## Scope
- Unit, integration, snapshot, PTY, browser-E2E, and script-driven end-to-end test layers.
- Rich fixture generation (multi-project, high-volume, pathological markdown, permission edge cases).
- Detailed artifact/logging strategy for debugging CI failures.
- Fault-injection and performance-regression scripts for hardening.
- Web UI parity suites against Python externally observed behavior.
- Static export conformance suites for GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages outputs.

## Mandatory Logging Contract
Every E2E/regression suite under this track must emit:
1. human-readable terminal/browser transcript,
2. structured metrics/traces JSON,
3. scenario metadata (seed/config/version/terminal profile/browser profile),
4. failure-focused diagnostics (diffs, stack traces, selected screen dumps, HTML snapshots when applicable).

## Outcome
A deterministic and explainable test matrix that captures correctness, UX regressions, parity regressions, and performance/reliability regressions with sufficient forensic detail to debug quickly.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit/integration/snapshot/PTY/browser-E2E/scripted-E2E suites are implemented and passing.
- Test suites emit transcript + metrics + trace + diagnostic artifact bundles.
- Failure triage is reproducible from captured artifacts.
- Coverage includes correctness, UX, parity (Python web behavior), performance, and fault-recovery behaviors.
- Static export outputs are validated for deterministic structure, working navigation, and search behavior on GH/CF Pages targets.\n- Test matrix includes widget-level unit coverage for the canonical widget catalog plus scripted breakpoint-cycling resize E2E with deterministic  {"id":"br-3vwi.7.5","title":"Implement SystemHealth + ToolMetrics observability surfaces with heatmaps and drill-downs","description":"## Purpose
Deliver explicit SystemHealth + ToolMetrics observability surfaces so operators can monitor runtime safety and per-tool performance without digging through raw logs.

## Scope
- SystemHealth panel set: circuit-breaker state, DB pool utilization, queue/backpressure indicators, and query latency distributions.
- ToolMetrics panel set: per-tool cards with call counts, latency ribbons, percentile bands, and error-rate badges.
- Query performance heatmap/timeline overlays for hotspot diagnosis.
- Drill-down paths from anomalous metric panels to correlated threads/messages/reservations.
- Capability-aware rendering for terminal profiles and multiplexer constraints.

## Why this matters
Duplicate beads (`br-134z.8*` + `br-134z.11*`) identified concrete visibility gaps. This bead keeps those exact operator outcomes in the canonical `br-3vwi` graph.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- SystemHealth and ToolMetrics surfaces are available as first-class, keyboard-navigable operator views.
- Metrics include deterministic formulas, sampling windows, percentile semantics, and confidence/context metadata.
